rationale: Both papers are in the loyalty/frequency-program domain, so the cited 2002 JMR article could plausibly serve as background or a conceptual source for customer behavior in programs. However, the provided evidence has only a DOI match (no in-text citation context), and the cited paper’s focus on effort-driven luxury vs. necessity reward preference is not clearly tied to the citing paper’s promotion-response decomposition, so it cannot be safely dropped or confidently kept.
